title: Go! Go! Ackman
titleNative: ゴーゴーアックマン
synopsis: Go! Go! Ackman is the story about a 200 year old demon child named Ackman who harvests souls for the Dark Devil Lord. Note: First released to public at 1994 V-Jump Festa, later it became a special video feature to be requested by V-Jump purchasers. (Source: Anime News Network, edited)

title: ARIEL VISUAL SCEBAI: Saidai no Kiki
titleRomaji: ARIEL VISUAL SCEBAI: Saidai no Kiki
titleNative: ARIEL VISUAL SCEBAI最大の危機
synopsis: To save the Earth from alien invaders and their giant monsters, Dr. Kishida does what any other red-blooded mad scientist would do: he builds a giant robot - ARIEL - the ultimate feminine fighting robot! Unfortunately, his grand-daughters refuse to pilot it! Apparently, they've got more important things to do than becoming teenage super-heroes. Besides, how are you supposed to study for your college entrance exams while getting beaten-up in battle? Fortunately for Dr. Kishida, things aren't too well for the invaders, either. According to their reports, the Earth was the perfect target: lush, peaceful, and relatively defenseless. Theoretically, the planet should've surrendered long ago. Now, the war is at a standstill, the dreaded Accounting Department is warning about the serious cost overruns, and the head of the home office has arrived from Galactic Headquarters to personally oversee operations. Has Headquarters decided to unleash, The Audit - or do they have something even more evil

title: Afro-Ken
titleNative: アフロ犬
synopsis: Afro Ken is a Japanese character created by the San-X company, which creates other anime-style characters such as Kogepan. He is a dog with a rainbow-coloured afro. He is innocent and blameless and always in a good mood. Afro Ken is always willing to try new things. Afro Ken is attracted to objects that look like he does. His hair changes to look like these things as he gets close to them. There is an Afro Ken Family Tree with other dog characters who have outrageous names, such as Techno Ken, Funky Ken, Lord Ken, Super Afro Ken, and Normal Ken. Unlike the others, Normal Ken does not have wild hair. (Source: Wikipedia)

title: Kidou Senshi SD Gundam Mk-V
titleNative: 機動戦士SDガンダム Mk-V
synonyms: Mobile Suit SD Gundam Mk. V
synopsis: In Episode 2, "Mononoke Chapter", the Musha Gundams attempt to solve the mystery of the apparition that haunts the town of Jaburo. Daishogun also has a treasure there... Episode 3 is split into three parts (construably considered episodes 3–5), and is titled "Gundam Book of Genesis" (ガンダム創世記, Gundam Sōseiki ). It revolves around a race of small, malleable Gundams and Zakus with designs based on haniwa (i.e. clay statues from Japan's Kofun period, ca. 300–538 CE). These creatures speak entirely in a fictitious language, and the story focuses on their small society's attempts to create new life.

title: Time Fighters
titleNative: タイムボカン
synonyms: Time Fighters in The Land of Fantasy
synopsis: Dr. Kieta, the inventor of a time-travel machine, is lost on a test run of his machine to the prehistoric past. When the time-space craft returns to the present, his young assistants are surprised to find only a parrot in the cockpit. In their hurry to find the missing scientist before it is too late, the youngsters set out on a time-space hunt without any clear destination. (Source: Official Site)

title: Tenbatsu Angel Rabbie☆
titleNative: 天罰エンジェルラビィ☆
synopsis: Long years after a war destroys most of the earth, Lasty Farson is one of the "angels" sent to points in history to set things right. Lasty is hopelessly clumsy and inept, but once she transforms into Angel Rabbie, she's the best there is. On her first day as a debut angel, she's late, but she is sent to earth and ends up battling old robots from the war--a tall order for a rookie angel. (Source: Anime News Network)
